---
name: design-guide
description: Creative design principles for distinctive, non-generic UI aesthetics. Use when designing or updating user interfaces to avoid AI slop and create delightful experiences.
applyTo: "**/*.{tsx,ts,css,scss}"
---

# Design Guide

## Typography
Choose beautiful, unique fonts that elevate the aesthetic. Avoid generic fonts like Arial, Inter, or Roboto. Opt for distinctive choices:
- Script fonts for headings (e.g., Dancing Script, Great Vibes)
- Elegant serif for body text (e.g., Crimson Text, Playfair Display)
- Pair with complementary sans-serif for UI elements

## Color & Theme
Commit to cohesive, bold aesthetics:
- Use dominant colors with sharp accents
- Draw inspiration from cultural themes (e.g., coffee shop: warm browns, creams)
- Avoid timid, evenly-distributed palettes
- Use CSS variables for consistency

## Motion & Animation
Add delight through thoughtful animations:
- Staggered reveals on page load
- Micro-interactions for user feedback
- CSS-only solutions preferred
- Focus on high-impact moments over scattered effects

## Backgrounds & Atmosphere
Create depth and context:
- Layered gradients instead of solid colors
- Geometric patterns or thematic elements
- Match overall aesthetic (e.g., coffee stains, steam effects)

## Avoid Generic AI Aesthetics
- Overused fonts and clichéd color schemes
- Predictable layouts
- Lack of character or context-specific design

## Implementation Notes
Match complexity to vision: Maximalist designs need elaborate code; minimalist designs need precision and restraint. Always think outside the box and commit fully to distinctive choices.
